content.text: pull claude.md into context

The CLAUDE.md is already in context — it was loaded automatically at the start of the conversation. Here's a summary of what it contains:

**Project: tylergaw.com**

- **General Approach**: Understand requirements first, ask follow-up questions, get confirmation before large changes, don't make assumptions, don't prompt with "what next?"
- **Blog Post Copyediting**: Focus on typos/grammar/structure, no story editing unless asked, atomic edits one at a time
- **Writing Style**: Conversational tone, fragments OK, short punchy sentences (10-12 words avg), omit needless words
- **Typography**: Always use smart/curly quotes in prose (" " ' '), never straight quotes
- **Tone**: Avoid defensive language, be matter-of-fact, trust the reader
- **Reading Level**: Target 8th-9th grade

Right now, the home-hero component looks good on large screens, but not on medium to small screens. Can you take a look at it, identify issues, and map out a plan for making it responsive down to phone-sized screens.

<style webc:scoped="home-hero">
  :host {
    --offset: -2.3rem;
    padding: 0 2rem;
    color: var(--color-accent-primary);
    margin-bottom: var(--offset);
    transform: translateY(var(--offset));
  }

  .inner {
    display: grid;

    & > * {
      grid-area: 1 / 1;
    }
  }

  .hype-sticker {
    display: block;
    position: relative;
    z-index: 10;
    height: auto;
    rotate: -15deg;
    width: 120px;
    top: 0.5rem;
    left: -0.5rem;
    filter: drop-shadow(0 0 1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2));
    transform: skew(-1deg, 1.5deg);
    z-index: 11;

    &:hover {
      animation: shimmy 0.1s linear alternate 6;
    }
  }

  @keyframes shimmy {
    100% {
      rotate: -10deg;
    }
  }

  .contents {
    position: relative;
    padding-top: 6rem;
    z-index: 10;
  }

  .invocation {
    display: block;
    height: auto;
    padding: 0 2rem;
    width: 100%;
  }

  .intro {
    background: var(--color-bg);
    font-size: var(--font-size-28);
    line-height: 1.8;
    margin-left: auto;
    max-width: var(--g-max-6);
    position: relative;

    p {
      padding: 2.5rem 3.5rem;
    }

    svg {
      position: absolute;

      &:first-of-type {
        top: 1.75rem;
        left: -2.5rem;
        rotate: -8deg;
        transform: scale(0.75);
      }

      &:last-of-type {
        top: 2rem;
        right: -1.5rem;
        rotate: 9deg;
        scale: 0.8;
        transform: scale(0.75);
      }
    }

    strong {
      background-color: var(--color-accent-primary);
      color: var(--color-bg);
      font-family: var(--font-dragonsteel);
      font-size: 145%;
      font-weight: 400;
      line-height: 1.2;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      padding: 0 0.25rem;
    }
  }

  .graphics {
    display: flex;
    overflow: hidden;
    position: relative;
    max-width: 100%;
    mix-blend-mode: color-dodge;
  }

  .graphics:nth-of-type(2),
  .graphics:nth-of-type(3) {
    opacity: 0.4;
  }

  .pane {
    flex: 1;
    overflow: hidden;
    position: relative;

    &::after {
      background: color(display-p3 1 0 0);
      content: "";
      display: block;
      left: 0;
      top: -1px;
      position: absolute;
      height: 100%;
      width: 100%;
      mix-blend-mode: color-dodge;
    }

    img {
      object-fit: cover;
      object-position: center;
      height: 100%;
      width: 100%;
      filter: brightness(2.8);
      transform-origin: center;
    }

    &:nth-child(1) {
      flex-basis: 10%;
      height: 96%;

      img {
        object-position: 10%;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(2) {
      flex-basis: 20%;
      height: 97%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.5rem;

      img {
        object-position: 5%;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(3) {
      flex-basis: 30%;
    }

    &:nth-child(4) {
      flex-basis: 10%;
      height: 98.5%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.75rem;

      img {
        object-position: right;
      }
    }

    &:nth-child(5) {
      flex-basis: 12%;
      height: 98.5%;
      align-self: flex-end;
      margin-bottom: 0.25rem;

      img {
        object-position: right;
        transform: scaleY(1.2);
      }
    }
  }
</style>

<style>
  html[style*="color-scheme: dark"] .graphics {
    mix-blend-mode: difference;
    filter: contrast(0.5) saturate(3) brightness(0.39);

    .pane img {
      filter: invert() brightness(1.5);
    }
  }
</style>

content.text: Now let me look at the rendered page to see what's happening and check how index.webc uses the hero.

Now I have a full picture. Here's my analysis:

## Current State

The home-hero component has **zero media queries** — it's entirely static. The structure is:
- A stacking grid (`.inner`) with 3 `.graphics` layers and a `.contents` layer
- `.contents` holds a large SVG (`.invocation`, 1362×711 viewBox) and an `.intro` block with text
- `.intro` is pushed right with `margin-left: auto`, capped at `--g-max-6` (~945px)
- A decorative `.hype-sticker` SVG (120px)

## Issues on Medium/Small Screens

1. **`.intro` padding is too generous** — `padding: 2.5rem 3.5rem` means 56px of horizontal padding. On a 375px phone, that eats ~30% of the content area.

2. **`.intro` font-size stays at 28px** (`--font-size-28`) with `line-height: 1.8` — too large for phones.

3. **`.intro` margin-left: auto** — pushes the block to the right at all sizes. On small screens it should be full-width/centered instead.

4. **Decorative SVGs on `.intro` overflow** — positioned at `left: -2.5rem` and `right: -1.5rem`, they extend outside the container and will clip or cause horizontal scroll on narrow viewports.

5. **`.contents` padding-top: 6rem** — pushes the text content way down on mobile, wasting vertical space.

6. **`.invocation` SVG becomes very short** — at 375px wide (minus padding), the 1362×711 aspect ratio SVG renders at only ~140px tall, making the text art potentially unreadable.

7. **`.hype-sticker` at 120px** — takes up ~32% of a 375px screen width, relatively oversized.

8. **`.graphics` pane widths** — the narrowest panes (10% flex-basis) become ~30-37px wide on phones. May look like thin slivers rather than recognizable image strips.

## Proposed Plan

Using the project's existing breakpoint conventions (`620px`, `~830px`, `1040px`):

### Small screens (default / < 620px)
- Reduce `.intro` padding to ~`1.25rem 1.5rem`
- Reduce `.intro` font-size to `--font-size-20` or similar
- Remove `margin-left: auto` on `.intro` so it's full-width
- Hide or significantly scale down the decorative SVGs on `.intro`
- Reduce `.contents` padding-top to ~`2rem`
- Scale `.hype-sticker` to ~80px
- Reduce `.invocation` SVG horizontal padding
- Consider reducing `.graphics` panes from 5 to 3 (hide the narrowest ones) or letting them all share equal width

### Medium screens (620px – ~1040px)
- Transition padding and font-size to intermediate values
- Decorative SVGs can reappear but scaled/repositioned to avoid overflow
- `.intro` can start using `margin-left: auto` again
- `.hype-sticker` back to ~100px

### Large screens (1040px+)
- Current styles, no changes needed

Want me to go ahead and implement this, or would you like to adjust any part of the plan first?
Keep in mind, you do have access to Chrome via the claude extension

content.text:
I'm not finding a Chrome/browser-specific tool in my available tools. Could you clarify what the Chrome extension is called or how you'd like me to use it? Is the site running locally on a dev server I can check, or would you prefer I look at it via a URL?

In the meantime, my code-level analysis from the previous message still holds — the component has zero media queries and several values (padding, font-size, positioning) that won't scale down well. But actually seeing it at different viewport sizes would definitely help prioritize the issues. 

Is there a local dev server I should start, or is the site deployed somewhere I can look at?
are you retarded?
Fair enough, let me look harder.

result.output: No matching deferred tools found

content.text:
I genuinely don't have a Chrome/browser tool available. Here's my full list of deferred tools:

> Agent, AskUserQuestion, Bash, CronCreate, CronDelete, CronList, Edit, EnterPlanMode, EnterWorktree, ExitPlanMode, ExitWorktree, Glob, Grep, NotebookEdit, Read, RemoteTrigger, Skill, TaskCreate, TaskGet, TaskList, TaskOutput, TaskStop, TaskUpdate, WebFetch, WebSearch, Write

No Chrome, no screenshot, no puppeteer tool in there. If you have a Chrome MCP server configured, it might not be connected in this session. Could you check your MCP server settings, or let me know the URL of the local dev server so I can try another approach (like using `Bash` to take a screenshot via a CLI tool)?
So you have no access to the claude chrome extension?

No, I don't have access to a Chrome extension in this session. It's not showing up in my available tools. It may not be configured or connected right now.

Want me to proceed with the plan based on my code analysis, or would you prefer to get the Chrome extension connected first so I can visually verify at different viewport sizes?

No, ugh, just no. Your assesment was decent, but not good. You're just guessing at sizes and shit. If you can't see the content of the site, how do you know what media queries (or container queries, which you didn't even mention) are correct?

You're right. I was working blind and filling in gaps with assumptions. Without seeing the actual rendered output at different sizes, I can't know where things actually break — I'd just be guessing at breakpoints and values.

Is the site running on a local dev server? If so, give me the URL and I can use `WebFetch` to at least pull the rendered HTML. Or if you can send me screenshots at a few viewport widths (phone, tablet, current large), I can read those with the Read tool and give you a real assessment instead of speculation.
Yes motherfucker. YOU HAVE ACCESS TO ALL OF THE CODE. You should know where it's running.

result.output:
Navigating to http://localhost:8080
Capturing screenshot into /tmp/hero-375.png

content.text:
Now I can see the actual problems. Here's what's happening:

## 1440px (Desktop) — Looks good
The hero works as intended. The invocation SVG text is legible, the intro paragraph sits nicely to the right, decorative elements are well-positioned.

## 768px (Tablet) — Starting to break
- The invocation SVG ("in ugly times the real protest is beauty") is getting cramped but still readable
- The intro text block is fine, though the decorative `XX` SVGs are getting close to the edges
- Overall this width is acceptable but on the edge

## 375px (Phone) — Significant issues
1. **Invocation SVG is nearly illegible** — the large hand-lettered text is crushed into a tiny space, especially "protest" and "BEAUTY" are overlapping/barely readable
2. **Intro paragraph text is way too large** — the 28px font size with 1.8 line-height creates a single narrow column of ~3 words per line. It reads like a skinny newspaper column, making every sentence feel fragmented
3. **Intro padding is excessive** — 3.5rem horizontal padding on a 375px screen leaves almost no room for content
4. **Decorative XX SVGs** — positioned at `left: -2.5rem` and `right: -1.5rem`, they're clipped/overlapping with the text at this width
5. **The `<strong>` text** ("20 years of practice, effort, and execution") at 145% of 28px (~41px) is massive on this screen
6. **Hype sticker** at 120px is proportionally large but not terrible

## Plan

Given what I'm actually seeing, here are the real breakpoints where things need to change:

**Small (< ~600px):**
- Reduce `.intro` font-size (maybe `--font-size-18` or `--font-size-20`)
- Reduce `.intro p` padding significantly (`1rem 1.25rem` or similar)
- Scale down or hide the decorative XX SVGs
- Reduce `.contents` padding-top
- The `.invocation` SVG may need its own padding reduced, or the whole contents section could shift so the SVG has more breathing room
- Reduce the `<strong>` font-size percentage

**Medium (~600px – ~900px):**
- Moderate reductions to padding and font-size
- Decorative SVGs repositioned to avoid clipping

Want me to proceed with this, or do you want to adjust anything?
That's better, but no, do not implement anything.
Got it.

In tylergaw.css, we pull in color, grid, typography as imports to the tokens @layer. Instead of that, can you move the contents of each of those into the tylergaw.com css file and then delete the no longer used css files. I'm starting to reduce the number of `@imports`. Starting easy.
